task_context.description: This is a systematic electromagnetic calorimeter performance study that investigates the relationship between detector depth and shower containment efficiency. The task involves conducting Monte Carlo simulations using Geant4 to model electromagnetic showers produced by electrons in a homogeneous lead tungstate (PbWO4) calorimeter. The study requires testing three different calorimeter depths (16, 20, and 25 radiation lengths) across multiple beam energies to quantify energy containment, resolution, and shower development characteristics. The investigation aims to establish design criteria for calorimeter depth by measuring energy leakage and determining the minimum thickness needed to achieve specific containment performance targets.
task_context.goal: Determine the minimum calorimeter depth required to keep energy leakage below 1% at 20 GeV and characterize how calorimeter depth affects electromagnetic shower containment across different energies
constraints: Energy leakage must be below 1% at 20 GeV
Use homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter material only
Run one depth configuration per geometry call

observations.content: This workflow systematically tests three calorimeter depths with energy sweeps to efficiently determine containment performance. The parallel energy sweeps minimize simulation time while providing comprehensive data for analysis. The analysis extracts key metrics (containment, resolution, shower max) needed to determine the minimum depth for <1% energy leakage at 20 GeV.
Step completed successfully: Generate GDML geometry for homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter with 16 radiation lengths depth (~14.2 cm)
Step completed successfully: Run Geant4 simulation with electron beam at multiple energies for 16 X0 depth calorimeter
Step completed successfully: Generate GDML geometry for homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter with 20 radiation lengths depth (~17.8 cm)
Step completed successfully: Run Geant4 simulation with electron beam at multiple energies for 20 X0 depth calorimeter
Step completed successfully: Generate GDML geometry for homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter with 25 radiation lengths depth (~22.3 cm)
Step completed successfully: Run Geant4 simulation with electron beam at multiple energies for 25 X0 depth calorimeter
Step completed successfully: Analyze energy containment fraction, resolution, and shower maximum position for all configurations
Step completed successfully: Create plots showing containment fraction vs depth for each energy, and determine minimum depth for <1% leakage at 20 GeV
Step completed successfully: Synthesize all results into comprehensive report on calorimeter depth optimization

## Executive Summary

This study investigated the relationship between electromagnetic calorimeter depth and shower containment efficiency using Monte Carlo simulations with Geant4. Three calorimeter depths (16, 20, and 25 radiation lengths) were tested with a homogeneous lead tungstate (PbWO4) detector across three beam energies (1, 5, and 20 GeV).
